name: Config file updated after cyoda:auth refreshes credentials
description: Verifies that the monitor detects a modification to an existing .cyoda/config file (e.g. when cyoda:auth overwrites the token) and notifies Claude without requiring the user to do anything.

input.environment: .cyoda/config already exists with a previous token; the user's session token has expired and they run cyoda:auth to refresh it
input.trigger: cyoda:auth overwrites .cyoda/config with an updated token field

expectedBehavior: Monitor detects the modification to the existing .cyoda/config file
Monitor emits: 'Cyoda config updated — run /cyoda:status to check connection'
Claude receives the notification and informs the user that credentials were refreshed
Claude suggests running /cyoda:status to confirm the connection is now active
antiPatterns: Monitor ignores modifications to an already-existing config file
Monitor emits a notification before the file is actually modified (false positive)
Claude is not notified even though the file changed on disk
